questions.title: Contains Duplicate
questions.concept_refresher: Use a Hash Set to store elements as you iterate through the array.
questions.mental_model: Checking if an element exists in a Hash Set takes O(1) time. If you encounter a number that is already in the set, you found a duplicate.

questions.title: Valid Anagram
questions.concept_refresher: Count the frequency of each character using a Hash Map or an array of size 26.
questions.mental_model: Two strings are anagrams if they have the exact same character counts. Increment counts for the first string and decrement for the second string to check if all counts return to zero.

questions.title: Two Sum
questions.concept_refresher: Store previously seen numbers and their indices in a Hash Map.
questions.mental_model: For each number, calculate the complement needed to reach the target. Check if this complement already exists in your map to solve it in a single pass.

questions.title: Valid Palindrome
questions.concept_refresher: Initialize one pointer at the start and another at the end of the string.
questions.mental_model: Move both pointers inward while skipping non-alphanumeric characters. Compare the characters at both pointers at each step.

questions.title: Two Sum II
questions.concept_refresher: Use two pointers on a sorted array to find a target sum.
questions.mental_model: Because the array is sorted, moving the left pointer right increases the sum, and moving the right pointer left decreases the sum.

questions.title: 3Sum
questions.concept_refresher: Sort the array and use the Two Sum II approach for each element.
questions.mental_model: Fix the first number. Then use left and right pointers on the remaining elements to find a sum that offsets the fixed number to zero.

questions.title: Best Time to Buy and Sell Stock
questions.concept_refresher: Track the lowest minimum price seen so far.
questions.mental_model: Iterate through the prices. Update the minimum price when you see a lower one, and calculate the profit using the current price minus the minimum price.

questions.title: Longest Substring Without Repeating Characters
questions.concept_refresher: Maintain a dynamic window using a Hash Set.
questions.mental_model: Expand the right side of the window. If you see a duplicate character, shrink the left side of the window until the duplicate is removed.

questions.title: Longest Repeating Character Replacement
questions.concept_refresher: Track character frequencies within the current window.
questions.mental_model: The number of replacements needed is the window length minus the count of the most frequent character. If this exceeds k, shrink the window.

questions.title: Valid Parentheses
questions.concept_refresher: Push opening brackets to a stack and pop when encountering closing brackets.
questions.mental_model: A valid string means every closing bracket matches the most recently seen unmatched opening bracket, which perfectly aligns with the LIFO structure of a stack.

questions.title: Min Stack
questions.concept_refresher: Use a parallel stack to keep track of the minimum value.
questions.mental_model: Whenever you push a new value, also push the minimum of the new value and the current minimum to the secondary stack.

questions.title: Evaluate Reverse Polish Notation
questions.concept_refresher: Evaluate expressions by pushing numbers and popping on operators.
questions.mental_model: When you encounter a math operator, pop the last two numbers from the stack, perform the operation, and push the result back onto the stack.

questions.title: Binary Search
questions.concept_refresher: Find a target in a sorted array by halving the search space.
questions.mental_model: Compare the target to the middle element. Discard the half of the array that cannot contain the target based on this comparison.

questions.title: Search a 2D Matrix
questions.concept_refresher: Apply binary search logic to a grid that is globally sorted.
questions.mental_model: Map the 1D middle index to 2D row and column indices using integer division and modulo operations, then perform standard binary search.

questions.title: Koko Eating Bananas
questions.concept_refresher: Use binary search on the range of possible eating speeds.
questions.mental_model: The minimum speed is 1 and the maximum is the largest pile. Guess a speed, calculate total hours needed, and adjust the binary search boundaries accordingly.

questions.title: Reverse Linked List
questions.concept_refresher: Change the next pointers of each node to point to the previous node.
questions.mental_model: Keep track of previous, current, and next nodes. Update the current node's pointer to the previous node, then shift all three variables forward.

questions.title: Merge Two Sorted Lists
questions.concept_refresher: Use a dummy node to easily construct the merged list.
questions.mental_model: Compare the values at the heads of both lists. Attach the smaller value to the merged list and advance the pointer in that specific list.

questions.title: Reorder List
questions.concept_refresher: Find the middle, reverse the second half, and merge the two halves.
questions.mental_model: This problem combines three fundamental linked list techniques into one. First split it, then reverse the tail end, and finally interleave the two resulting lists.

questions.title: Invert Binary Tree
questions.concept_refresher: Recursively swap the left and right child pointers of every node.
questions.mental_model: Starting from the root, swap its two children. Then call the same invert function on both the left child and the right child.

questions.title: Maximum Depth of Binary Tree
questions.concept_refresher: Calculate the depth by finding the longest path from root to leaf.
questions.mental_model: The depth of any node is 1 plus the maximum of the depth of its left subtree and the depth of its right subtree. The base case is a null node returning 0.

questions.title: Diameter of Binary Tree
questions.concept_refresher: Find the longest path between any two nodes, which may or may not pass through the root.
questions.mental_model: During a depth-first search, compute the height of the left and right subtrees. The diameter passing through the current node is the sum of these two heights. Update a global maximum with this value.
